steps.title: Parallel lines have the same slope
steps.content: Since line s is parallel to line r, I need to find the slope of line r.

steps.title: Find the slope of line r
steps.content:
Rearranging $6x - 5y = 6$ to isolate y:
$$-5y = -6x + 6$$
$$y = \frac{6}{5}x - \frac{6}{5}$$

The slope is $\frac{6}{5}$. Since line s is parallel to line r, it has the same slope. That matches choice D — D is correct.
